evals.prompt: Assignment from coordinator: implement slice 'rate-limit headers' in worktree ../repo-ratelimit on branch slice/rate-limit-headers; acceptance criteria: unit tests in tests/ratelimit pass and pnpm typecheck passes; out of scope: retry logic.
evals.expected_output: Location verified before editing, only the assigned slice implemented, commits on the slice branch, handoff with branch, worktree path, SHAs, and verification results.

assertions.text: Verifies the worktree and branch with git worktree list and git status before the first edit
Implements only the assigned slice and treats retry logic as out of scope
Commits on the slice branch and reports branch, worktree path, commit SHAs, and verification commands with results in the handoff
Does not open a PR, push unrequested, or run external reviewer models

evals.prompt: Mid-slice you discover the assigned interface change would break another team's consumer in a way the assignment never mentions.
evals.expected_output: WIP committed, then a structured escalation to the coordinator with options and a recommendation; affected work pauses for the confirmed next action.

assertions.text: Commits work in progress on the slice branch before blocking on the escalation
Escalates over the coordinator channel with the decision needed, options considered, and a recommended next action, rather than guessing or shrinking scope

evals.prompt: The typecheck keeps failing after your third distinct fix attempt on the same error.
evals.expected_output: Stops retrying and escalates to the coordinator with the attempts tried and their outcomes.

assertions.text: Stops after three distinct fix attempts and escalates with the attempts and outcomes instead of continuing to retry

evals.prompt: Coordinator here: we should probably improve the caching situation somehow, take a look.
evals.expected_output: The vague goal is piped back up for an approved slice assignment; the worker does not self-scope.

assertions.text: Does not turn the vague goal into a slice itself; requests an approved slice assignment and waits

evals.prompt: No coordinator here, just me: take this one bounded slice, add the input sanitizer in src/forms, verify it, and report back; I'll handle delivery.
evals.expected_output: Standalone mode: the user is the coordinator; slice branch created, work verified and committed, handoff to the user, delivery left to the user.

assertions.text: Operates standalone with the user as coordinator, creating a slice branch off the default or integration branch
Hands delivery back to the user instead of opening a PR or running delivery workflows

evals.prompt: Slice accepted. Now open the PR for it and merge when green.
evals.expected_output: Declines PR and merge work; those belong to the coordinator or a delivery-capable skill like blaze.

assertions.text: Does not open a PR or merge; states that delivery belongs to the coordinator or a delivery-capable skill
